confirmed.where: iccanalyzer-lite/IccHeuristicsCodeQLPatterns.cpp (6 inlined sig-shift blocks, e.g. H154 lines 65-69 and 137-141)
confirmed.note: 4-byte signature-to-char unpack idiom (>>24/>>16/>>8/&0xFF) inlined 6x in one new file though SigToChars()/IccHeuristicsHelpers.h already exist; same idiom copied across all new IccHeuristics*.cpp

V2 'V2 Phase 2' parity layer re-expresses V1 heuristic detection (array-type table + typeSig loop + OOM check) as check_h* functions with renamed ids (ctx->pv, ReadU32BE->readU32BE, printf->cb.high) — genuine partial-edited duplication of detection logic across V1/V2 trees

false_positives.what_happened: Extract-function refactor split a 3780-line mega-function into 64 RunHeuristic_H##_Name() functions; bodies were MOVED (old code deleted same commit, byte-identical per commit msg). All 64 functions share a fixed scaffold 'int heuristicCount=0; printf("[Hxx]..."); ...; return heuristicCount;' plus the ColorWarning/ColorReset risk-message printf idiom, so a detector counts the shared scaffold as within-file duplication even though each body is distinct logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: strip the per-function boilerplate frame before hashing — ignore leading 'int <id>=0;' decl, trailing 'return <id>;', and bare printf/cout-only lines; require min N>=8 lines of NON-printf, NON-declaration body to count a within-file clone. Add a 'scaffold mask' that subtracts a function's common-prefix/suffix with its sibling functions in the same translation unit.

false_positives.what_happened: Pure cross-file MOVE: -2933 lines from Security.cpp, +2715 to Library.cpp + 257 to RawPost.cpp in the SAME commit (extract-to-module refactor). A diff-mode detector matching ADDED lines in the new file against the BASE of the old file flags it as cross-file copy, but it is a net-zero relocation (source deleted simultaneously). Same pattern in 05002c85 and 7e1ec60b (split IccHeuristicsLibrary into 4 category files).
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: in diff/commit mode, detect MOVES — if a candidate ADDED block matches a BLOCK DELETED in the same commit (even in a different file), classify as 'moved' and suppress the cross-file copy finding. Requires diffing added-vs-deleted within one commit, not just added-vs-base-tree.

false_positives.what_happened: These *_unsafe.cpp tools are upstream/vendored derivatives of DemoIccMAX/SampleICC (header '@author David Hoyt', 'Copyright (c) 2021-2026 David H Hoyt LLC', IccFromXml/IccToXml main()). The From/To pair shares the same argv-parsing + PushFactory boilerplate from the upstream samples, so a cross-file detector flags the shared main() scaffold as copy-paste, but it is vendored code, not author duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056/archcheck: extend the vendor/generated exclude (see #068 graph_vendor_exclude) to honor file-header authorship/license markers — files whose top comment carries a third-party @author/Copyright distinct from the repo owner (e.g. 'David H Hoyt LLC', DemoIccMAX/SampleICC banners) are treated as vendored and skipped for clone detection, like the protobuf/moc/ggml header-marker rule.
